title: Object dispenser having a variable orifice and image identification
abstract: A method and apparatus for dispensing objects from automated storage and retrieval systems such as medications is disclosed which may also include a singulator to assure singulation (retrieval of singular objects). The singulator may be an imaging system to confirm and/or identify the objects being dispensed and/or a variable orifice dynamically adjustable to a specific object or pill size, and through which only a single object or pill is allowed to pass. The system may further include a flexible probe so as to reduce the size of the system where the movement of the probe relative to or while retrieving objects is accomplished by advancing/retracting a flexible tube. The method and apparatus may further include an imaging system.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A dispensing method comprising: providing a dispensing system for dispensing at least one of a plurality of objects stored in containers therein; said system, in response to a dispense request, retrieving an object using a probe extending downward into a container to collect an object therefrom and transferring the object to a dispense location accessible by a user; and storing and maintaining an inventory level for each of the objects stored in the dispensing system. 2. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , wherein the occurrence of a possible container refill operation is detected as a result of a change in the depth of probe downward deployment between sequential accesses of a particular container. 3. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , wherein a change in inventory quantity is detected as a result of a change in the level of objects in a particular container. 4. The dispensing method according to claim 3, wherein the change is detected as a result of an analysis of at least two images of the container acquired at different times. 5. The dispensing method according to claim 3, wherein dimensional features of the object are characterized in response to an image of the object acquired by the dispensing system at at least one location in the system. 6. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , further including acquiring at least one image of an object container, and based, at least in part, upon information obtained from said image, estimating an approximate inventory level for objects in the object container. 7. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , wherein said dispensing system further includes a communication interface, and a separate device in communication with the interface that is capable of acquiring an image of a collection of pills to stock or restock a container in the dispensing system, and where the separate device determines and provides at least a pill count to the dispensing system. 8. The dispensing method according to claim 7, wherein counting of objects further includes: sending a message to the separate device to identify and count objects; identifying known and unknown objects within a collection of objects; and locating identification markings or attributes on the objects. 9. The dispensing method according to claim 7, wherein counting is facilitated regardless of whether the objects are spread in a single layer or are piled so as to include multiple layers. 10. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , wherein the operation of storing and maintaining an inventory level for each of the objects stored in the dispensing system further includes: picking several objects to determine an accurate average weight per object; and estimating the inventory on-hand based upon a weight of objects in a container divided by the average weight. 11 . The dispensing method according to claim 1 , wherein the operation of storing and maintaining an inventory level further includes dispensing an inventory of objects until the last object has been dispensed to assure all objects of a particular load have been depleted. 12. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , wherein the dispensing system further includes at least one weighing station where an object or an object container may be placed to sense the object weight. 13. The dispensing method according to claim 12, wherein the tare weight of the empty container is stored and where the total weight of objects in the container is determined by subtracting the tare weight from the weight of the container with objects. 14. The dispensing method according to claim 13 wherein a container's weight may be assessed by rotating a carousel on which a container is placed to align the container with a weighing station, and removing the container from the carousel support. 15. The dispensing method according to claim 1 , further including visual identification of an includes sensing at least one object attribute selected from the group consisting of: size, shape, color, quantity, weight, visual markings, and spectroscopy to provide object identification. 16. An object dispensing system, including: a housing comprising a rotatable carousel below a support frame, the rotatable carousel including a plurality of individually removable containers where each container provides a repository for objects therein; an access port in the support frame of the housing through which an object may be removed from each of the plurality of containers, in seriatim; a pre-dispense port in the support frame of the housing providing controlled access to one of a plurality of output containers; and a retrieval probe, said probe located within the confines of the housing and operatively sliding between the access port and the pre-dispense port, said probe including a compliant tip for releasably engaging an object in one of the plurality of containers via said access port, said probe retrieving the object from a container and then moving between the access port and the pre-dispense port to deliver the object to a dispensing receptacle via the pre-dispense port. 17. The object dispensing system according to claim 16, further including memory for storing and maintaining an inventory level for the objects in the containers. 18. The object dispensing system according to claim 16, further comprising an image sensing device for obtaining an image of a container in order to determine the location of an object therein and to move the probe relative to the container to target the object for retrieval. 19. The object dispensing system according to claim 18, wherein a visual identification of an object includes using at least one object attribute selected from the group consisting of: size, shape, color, quantity, weight, visual markings, and spectroscopy to provide an identification of the object. 20. The object dispensing system according to claim 16 wherein the retrieval probe is a flexible probe. 21. A medicament dispensing method comprising: providing a dispensing system, including a housing having a rotatable carousel therein with a plurality of removable open-top containers, where the carousel moves under the control of a carousel drive, each container including a repository for objects therein; an access port in the housing through which an object may be removed from the plurality of containers, in seriatim; a pre-dispense port in the housing providing controlled access to one of a plurality of output containers; and a retrieval probe, said probe located within the housing and operatively movable under the control of a horizontal slide drive by a slide motor between the access port and the pre-dispense port, said probe further including a vertical slide retracting 

 and extending the probe and a compliant tip thereon under control of a vertical slide motor, the probe tip releasably engaging an object in one of the plurality of containers via the access port, placing a different medicament into at least two of the plurality of containers; in response to a dispense request, a controller operating to move the carousel to place a container having the requested medicament beneath the access port, and moving the horizontal probe slide into a position so that the probe tip is over the access port, wherein the probe is then extended to engage a medicament, and once engaged by the probe tip, the medicament is raised while attached to the probe tip and retrieved from the container via the access port; said controller then engaging the horizontal slide drive to cause the horizontal probe slide to move into a position over the pre-dispense port; releasing the medicament from the probe tip to deliver the medicament to a pre-dispense receptacle beneath the pre-dispense port; moving the pre-dispense receptacle, using a pre-dispense slide motor responsive to the controller, to transfer the medicament from the pre-dispense receptacle to a dispense cup accessible by a user; in the event of a failure to retrieve medicament from said dispense cup, moving said medicament to a restock compartment; and pre-dispensing and automatically loading a portable dispenser operatively associated with said restock compartment location through a door opened therein. 22. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 21 , further comprising a portable dispenser in the form of a stand-alone device with a plurality of labeled, covered, lockable, and moisture resistant, compartments accessible to a user upon removal from the restock compartment. 23. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 22, wherein said portable dispenser is in temporary contact with the said dispensing system and further in communication (wired or wireless) with said system. 24. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 23, wherein said portable dispenser is at least temporarily positioned in the dispense area or another auxiliary location (in our design will be in restock compartment location with load door opened), and where a plurality of compartments in the portable dispenser are positioned in a load position and loaded with a medicament(s) dose. 25. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 24, wherein said portable dispenser includes a plurality of movable compartments. 26. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 25, wherein said portable dispenser includes a central motor and processer (or is placed on a movable base) for loading with base with motor to rotate or index the portable dispenser compartments relative to the dispense location. 27. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 23, wherein said portable dispenser includes memory and a processor suitable to receiving, storing and operating in accordance with information received via communication with the system, or via communication with a server or portal via the system. 28. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 27, wherein after loading the portable dispenser is removed from said dispenser system and operates to dispense a dose by signaling a compartment to open (e.g., audio, light, display, tactile indicator) or otherwise moves (e.g. rotates a 47 RECTIFIED (RULE 91) - ISA/US 

 portable dispenser compartment to a dispense location), unlocks and dispenses pills from a selected compartment(s). 29. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 28, further including a sensor to confirm the compartment empty once dispensed. 30. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 23, wherein said portable dispenser also communicates messages (same as dispenser). 31 . The medicament dispensing method according to claim 30, wherein said portable dispenser includes a communication channel for communication with one or more remote devices selected from the group consisting of: wireless connection with a (patient's) cell phone; wireless communication (e.g., Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, RF, etc.), and wired (e.g. modem) 32. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 23, wherein said portable dispenser devices are stackable (or packable into case) so that a patient can store multiple periods (e.g. weeks) of medicaments for travel. 33. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 32, wherein when travel is complete the medicaments in any unused portable dispenser device(s) compartment(s) may be returned to the dispenser system for restocking of unused meds (either manually or automatically). 34. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 23, wherein said portable dispenser further includes a schedule control feature that enables dispensing from only one of either the dispensing system or the portable dispenser device(s) in order to prevent double dosing of a medicament(s). 35. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 33, wherein said portable dispenser devices may be of a size suitable for the period of medicament dosage (e.g., smaller, single or few compartments for a single or few dosages, larger, 1 -week size, and stackable or combinable for multi-day/multi-week dispensing) 36. An object dispensing system, including: a housing comprising a rotatable carousel, the rotatable carousel including a plurality of removable containers where each container provides a repository for objects therein and wherein each container is open at the top and at least one removable cover operatively covers the containers; an access port in the housing through which an object may be removed from each of the plurality of containers, in seriatim; a pre-dispense port in the housing providing controlled access to one of a plurality of output containers; and a retrieval probe, said probe located within the confines of the housing and operatively moving between the access port and the pre-dispense port, said probe including a compliant tip for releasably engaging an object in one of the plurality of containers via said access port, said probe retrieving the object from a container and then moving between the access port and the pre-dispense port to deliver the object to a dispensing receptacle. 37. The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein said removable cover can be moved relative to at least one container that it covers to provide access to the at least one container by said retrieval probe. 38. The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein said at least one removable cover remains in a sealing relationship with a respective container until such time as the cover is opened for retrieval of an object from the respective container in order to prevent inadvertent mixing or spillage from a container. 39. The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein the at least one removable cover remains in a sealing relationship with the respective container and thereby provides a moisture-proof seal. 40. The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein the at least one removable cover remains in a sealing relationship with the respective container and thereby provides an airtight seal. 41 . The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein the at least one removable cover includes one piece cover over each container and where said removable cover that is movable (translate, rotate) to allow access to at least one container. 42. The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein the at least one removable cover includes an opening with a movable diaphragm (or similar) to allow probe access, and upon removal of the probe the diaphragm is resealed. 43. The object dispensing system of claim 36, wherein the at least one removable cover includes a single cover over a plurality of containers on said carousel, said removable cover further including a single opening that may be moved to a position over a container to allow access thereto. 44. The object dispensing system of claim 43, wherein the at least one removable cover covers all of the containers on the carousel. 45. An object dispensing system, including: a housing comprising a rotatable carousel, the rotatable carousel including a plurality of removable containers where each container provides a repository for objects therein and wherein each container includes an identifier that is unique within the system; an access port in the housing through which an object may be removed from each of the plurality of containers, in seriatim; a pre-dispense port in the housing providing controlled access to one of a plurality of output containers; and a retrieval probe, said probe located within the confines of the housing and operatively moving between the access port and the pre-dispense port, said probe including a compliant tip for releasably engaging an object in one of the plurality of containers via said access port, said probe retrieving the object from a container and then moving between the access port and the pre-dispense port to deliver the object to a dispensing receptacle. 46. The dispensing system according to claim 45, wherein the unique identifier associated with each container (bin) is provided from one of a group of identification techniques consisting of a label, tag or clip. 47. The dispensing system according to claim 45, wherein the unique identifier associated with each container (bin) is provided from one of a group of identification techniques consisting of: magnetic code, optical code (e.g., barcode), physical feature or mark, and radio-frequency code. 48. The dispensing system according to claim 45, wherein the plurality of removable containers includes containers of different sizes (volumes) adjacent one another on said carousel. 49. The dispensing system according to claim 48, wherein the plurality of removable containers each include a registration feature suitable for indicating a relative position of the container on said carousel. 50. The dispensing system according to claim 49, wherein the registration feature is selected from the group consisting of protrusions, tic marks, notches, container edges, tags on container edges, windows in a cover of the container. 51 . The dispensing system of claim 50, wherein said registration features are placed along at least one side, edge or surface of the containers. 52. The dispensing system of claim 50, wherein the containers include med bin location e.g. auto med location, where each bin or container is dispensed from the pharmacy with a barcode or similar identifier placed on it. 53. The dispensing system according to claim 50, wherein each of the plurality of containers includes at least two registration features. 54. The dispensing system according to claim 45, further including a container advancement mechanism whereby when a container is selected for use on the carousel, the carousel is rotated to place the container in a desired location and the container is subsequently moved relative to said carousel. 55. The dispensing system according to claim 54, where movement of the selected container is accomplished using a mechanical assembly selected from the group consisting of plunger/solenoid, magnet, door closing, snaps, closing cover (e.g., lift ring) to position containers on the carousel, limited vertical clearance to ensure slot engagement. 56. The dispensing system according to claim 49, further including a detector for sensing the identification and/or registration feature. 57. The dispensing system according to claim 56, wherein said detector senses the identification and/or registration feature as the container moves relative to the detector. 58. The dispensing system according to claim 57, wherein the relative position of each of the plurality of containers is detected upon rotation of said carousel. 59. The dispensing system according to claim 57, wherein the identification and/or registration feature is detected upon movement of said container relative to the detector. 60. The dispensing system according to claim 57, wherein the detector includes a device for capturing an optical image. 61 . The dispensing system according to claim 60, wherein the detector includes a fiber optic component. 62. The dispensing system according to claim 57, wherein said identification and/or registration feature is detected as a single image view. 63. The dispensing system according to claim 57, wherein said identification and/or registration feature is detected as a plurality of image views. 64. A medicament dispensing method comprising: providing a dispensing system, including a housing having a rotatable carousel therein with a plurality of removable open-top containers, where the carousel moves under the control of a carousel drive, each container including a repository for objects therein; an access port in the housing through which an object may be removed from the plurality of containers, in seriatim; a pre-dispense port in the housing providing 

 controlled access to one of a plurality of output containers; and a retrieval probe, said probe located within the housing and operatively movable under the control of a horizontal slide drive by a slide motor between the access port and the pre-dispense port, said probe further including a vertical slide retracting and extending the probe and a compliant tip thereon under control of a vertical slide motor, the probe tip releasably engaging an object in one of the plurality of containers via the access port, placing a different medicament into at least two of the plurality of containers; in response to a dispense request, a controller operating to move the carousel to place a container having the requested medicament beneath the access port, and moving the horizontal probe slide into a position so that the probe tip is over the access port, wherein the probe is then extended to engage a medicament, and once engaged by the probe tip, the medicament is raised while attached to the probe tip and retrieved from the container via the access port, further including moving said carousel (container) and probe relative to one another to facilitate the probe contacting a medicament; said controller then engaging the horizontal slide drive to cause the horizontal probe slide to move into a position over the pre-dispense port; releasing the medicament from the probe tip to deliver the medicament to a pre-dispense receptacle beneath the pre-dispense port; and moving the pre-dispense receptacle, using a pre-dispense slide motor responsive to the controller, to transfer the medicament from the pre-dispense receptacle to a dispense cup accessible by a user. 65. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, further comprising a dithering pattern for moving the probe relative to the container, said pattern intended to locate any medicament remaining in said container. 66. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, further including adjustment of the vacuum pressure applied to said probe (e.g., increase the vacuum pressure applied to the retrieval tip). 67. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, further comprising moving the probe arcuately (along an arc) relative to the container, said pattern intended to locate any medicament remaining in said container and to move any undetected medicament. 68. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, further comprising using an image sensing device (e.g., camera) and obtaining an image of the container in order to determine the location(s) of a medicament and to move the probe relative to the container to target the medicament. 69. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 68, wherein the location(s) of a medicament is includes both horizontal and vertical (e.g., 3D) position components. 70. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 68, wherein the image is analyzed based on optical/visual attributes of the medicaments to determine the oldest medicament in the field of view and targeting the oldest medicament, if detected. 71 . The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, wherein at least one medicament includes an over-encapsulated medicament form (e.g., partial tablet or combination placed into capsule for dispensing) in order to improve probe picking consistency and preventing the probe from collecting medicament fragments, (e.g., caregiver/pharmacy splits pills and loads into capsules - or automated process) 72. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, wherein said probe tip is resilient, (e.g., spring relief/release mechanism on probe end) 73. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, wherein the probe may be moved relative to a pharmacy container (in a separate pick location or in a container position on said carousel), and where said probe retrieves the medicament directly from the pharmacy container. 74. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 73, further comprising obtaining an image of the container and performing at least one recognition operation (e.g., word recognition, bar code recognition) in order to determine the contents of the pharmacy container as indicated by a label thereon. 75. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, wherein the dispensing system includes the ability to receive, store and retrieve medicaments directly from at least one pharmacy container. 76. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 75, wherein the method further includes automatically removing a cap (cover) from a pharmacy container(s) prior to medicament retrieval and restoring a removed cap after retrieval, (e.g., mechanical system to move variety of shaped bottles to picking location (or probe moves to bottle location), either load without caps or mechanical device to remove (and replace caps), pick directly from bottles after reading labels for instruction.) 77. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 68, wherein the image is analyzed, based on optical/visual attributes, to detect a desiccant (package) within a container (e.g., if accidentally added to bin when pills were loaded), and to subsequently use the image sensing system to target and remove the desiccant to a disposal location, or to avoid picking the desiccant and display a message to assure disposal of the desiccant upon a subsequent refill of the associated container. 78. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 77, wherein the method further comprises, placing a detected desiccant in a temporary holding location. 79. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 68, wherein the acquired container image(s) is analyzed via optical identification and the results are used to avoid picking medicaments that do not match the loaded pill attributes. 80. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 79, wherein medicaments that do not match the loaded attributes are picked and moved to an alternative location (such as restock), and the system communicates this occurrence via a message (i.e., informs caregiver). 81 . The medicament dispensing method according to claim 68, wherein visual identification of a medicament includes using at least one pill attribute from the group consisting of: size, shape, color, quantity, weight, visual markings, spectroscopy, to provide an identification (e.g., NDC, name, number, etc.) and may further include a verification operation such as, human visual confirmation. 82. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 81 , wherein the method further includes the collection of pill attribute data along with the storage of such data at the time a medicament is loaded into the system, and where at least a portion of the stored data is employed for detecting a match(es) in a subsequent visual identification operation. 83. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 82, wherein at least one attribute of the medicament is obtained via a remote device (e.g., smart phone, tablet, etc.) running an application and wherein the application acquires images and other attributes of the medicament and communicates such information to the system. 84. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 83, further comprising the system, in the event of a new medicament not previously stored in the system, confirming the medicament using one or more attributes. 85. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, wherein any medicament(s) that is not dispensed (e.g., remaining in pre-dispense location or dispense cup) undergoes a visual identification and is returned to the container from which it was retrieved. 86. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, further comprising singulation of the medicament. 87. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 86, wherein singulation includes impact of at least a second medicament that remains contact with the probe tip or a first medicament upon retrieval of the tip from a container but is prevented from being retrieved by contact (e.g., closing a variable orifice, touching container side, downward displacement of probe into container, etc.). 88. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 86, wherein an imaging (machine and/or human visual confirmation), operation is performed to identify the presence of the second medicament in association with the probe tip or the first medicament. 89. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 86, wherein singulation is determined by sensing the level of the vacuum draw on the probe tip, wherein a vacuum pressure of less than a predefined pressure indicates an incomplete connection between the probe tip and the medicament and a possible multiple-medicament situation. 90. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 86, wherein singulation is determined by sensing the weight of a container both before and after retrieval of a medicament, and where a difference in weight is calculated and compared to a known medicament weight for the medicament in the container, and singulation is confirmed when the weight is within the range of a single medicament. 91 . The medicament dispensing method according to claim 64, further comprising determining how full a container is. 92. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 91 , wherein determining how full a container is includes obtaining at least one image of the container and based upon a volumetric calculation estimating the remaining number of medicaments in the container. 93. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 91 , wherein determining how full a container is includes obtaining the weight of a container and conducting upon a calculation estimating the remaining number of medicaments in the container based upon the measured weight less a tare weight for the container. 94. A medicament dispensing method comprising: providing a dispensing system for dispensing at least one of a plurality of medicaments stored therein; said system, in response to a dispense request, retrieving a medicament and transferring the medicament to a dispense location accessible by a user; and providing a communication channel between the dispensing system and a remote device. 95. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communication channel is used to transfer information related to system configuration. 96. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communication channel is used by the device to initiate contact with at least one caregiver in accordance with a pre-defined calling hierarchy. 97. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 96, wherein the methods of contact facilitated by the communication channel include e-mail, text (SMS), voice, etc. 98. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 97, wherein at least one notification method is specified for each caregiver in the hierarchy, and an event detected by the dispensing system resulting in a notification requirement causes the dispensing system to communicate to the caregiver in the at least one notification method. 99. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communication channel further facilitates handling of a response to communication sent from the dispensing system. 100. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 99, wherein a communication initiated by the dispensing system indicates that a response is required. 101 . The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communication channel facilitates the sending of general information including information selected from the group consisting of: health tips, activities, videos, audio files from family/friends/care providers others, etc. 102. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 96, wherein the contact includes a specific notification (e.g. late dose, missed, machine tampering, fault reporting) 103. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communication channel facilitates exchange of information between the patient and the dispensing system, and the dispensing system preparing and sending at least one location-based notification based upon the patient's remote location relative to the dispensing system. 104. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein a patient's position is determined as a result of a location provided via the patient's phone, in communication with the dispensing system or portal, and where when the patient's phone is determined to be greater than a local distance away (e.g., within home or facility boundary), the dispensing system causes an alert to be communicated to the patient's phone reminding the patient of an upcoming dosage. 105. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 104, wherein the time at which the reminder is sent is based on estimated travel time between the location of the patient's phone and the dispensing system (e.g. head for home now to stay on schedule) 106. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 104, wherein the system initiates a caregiver communication in response to the detection of a patient's position (e.g. your mom is too far away and will not be there for the 6:00 pm dose). 107. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communication channel is used to transfer information related to a refill reminder, and where the communication is based upon the dispensing system monitoring medicament inventory, depth, expiration, etc. 108. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the dispensing system records a history of dispensing "as needed" medicaments and the communication channel is used to initiate a notification for 'as needed' medicaments based on recent use history. 109. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 94, wherein the communications channel is further employed, along with one or more sensors, cameras, and user interface devices (e.g., display) associated with the dispensing system or portal to facilitate communications between a patient and caregiver as a telemedicine hub. 110. A medicament dispensing method comprising: providing a dispensing system for dispensing at least one of a plurality of medicaments stored therein; said system, in response to a dispense request, retrieving a medicament and transferring the medicament to a dispense location accessible by a user; and confirming the medicament dosage by obtaining an image of the dispense location before dispensing. 11 1 . The medicament dispensing method according to claim 1 10, wherein dispensing of the medicament dosage is confirmed by obtaining an image of the dispense location after dispensing to confirm all medicaments were retrieved. 112. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 1 10wherein the dispensing system includes a communication channel and is capable of initiating med-linked reminders. 113. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 1 10, further including acquiring at least one image of a medicament dispensed by the system and automatically processing the acquired image (oriented/magnified/centered) for display and use by the system and user. 114. The medicament dispensing method according to claim F4, wherein the dispensing system performs an image comparison between the loaded medicament(s) and a medicament database, and in the event of any discrepancy (e.g., unable to confirm match), acquiring an image of the medicaments in the dispense location. 115. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 1 10, further including determining, based upon an image acquired (preferably from medicament storage container, but no later than at dispensing location), information about the medicament to be dispensed that has been stored in the system and further confirming that the expiration date of the medicament has not passed prior to dispensing the medicament. 116. The medicament dispensing method according to claim 110, further including the acquisition of an image of each medicament type during or prior to loading of the medicament into a container for storage in the dispensing system. 117. A medicament dispensing system, including: a housing comprising a carousel, the carousel including a plurality of removable containers where each container provides a repository for medicaments therein; a retrieval probe, said probe located within the confines of the housing and operatively moving between an access port to at least one container and the pre-dispense port, said probe releasably engaging a medicament in one of the plurality of containers via said access port, retrieving the medicament from the container and delivering the medicament to a dispensing location; and an interface operatively associated with the dispensing system or portal whereby the dispensing system may be associated with at least one patient. 118. The medicament dispensing system of claim 1 17, wherein the interface enables registration of the dispensing system to a portal, and where said portal includes patient information including medicament dispense instructions to be executed by the dispensing system. 119. The medicament dispensing system of claim 1 17, wherein the interface enables association of a caregiver (aide, family member, doctor, pharmacist, etc.) to the patient's dispensing system and thereby facilitates a caregiver to monitor the performance of the dispensing system, including the patient's retrieval of dispensed medicaments.. 120. The medicament dispensing system of claim 1 19, further including with said interface an authorization feature whereby the information that is released to a caregiver may be controlled by the caregiver's position (e.g., doctor may receive more information than an aide). 121 . The medicament dispensing system of claim 1 18, wherein the interface, and access to the dispensing system or its information via the portal may be controlled (enabled, disabled, suspended, time limited, etc.) via the interface. 122. The medicament dispensing system of claim 1 18, wherein the interface is a feature of the dispensing system. 123. The medicament dispensing system of claim 118, wherein the interface is a feature of the portal. 124. A medicament dispensing system, including: a housing comprising a carousel, the carousel including a plurality of removable containers where each container provides a repository for medicaments therein; a retrieval probe, said probe located within the confines of the housing and operatively moving between an access port to at least one container and the pre-dispense port, said probe releasably engaging a medicament in one of the plurality of containers via said access port, retrieving the medicament from the container and delivering the medicament to a dispensing location; and a scheduling interface operatively associated with the dispensing system or portal to enter and display complex dosing schedules (e.g., display including a visual representation of the schedule). 125. The medicament dispensing system according to claim 124, further including a communication channel (wired connection (e.g., USB), wireless connection (wifi, Bluetooth), etc.) whereby a personal computing device (e.g., smartphone, tablet, etc.) can access the dispensing system and the scheduling interface for review and editing of the medicament list and the dosing schedule. 126. The medicament dispensing system according to claim 124, further including access to a medicament library of information in order to provide, in response to an entry to the dosing schedule, an indication of information selected from the group consisting of: warning (e.g., drug conflicts or interaction, patient drug allergy, etc.) and information regarding the patient's pharmacogenomics. 127. The medicament dispensing system according to claim 124, wherein said scheduling interface further includes confirmation of medicine programming (.g., today's programming (or dispense list) is different from what it was yesterday or monitoring an escalation/taper/completed med. 128. The medicament dispensing system according to claim 124, wherein the dispensing system further includes an interface (e.g., manual entry, sensor, imaging camera) for the collection of real-time patient information, and wherein said scheduling interface may operatively adjust a dosage or dispensing schedule in response to such real-time patient information. 129. The medicament dispensing system according to claim 128, wherein the dosage adjustment may be based on patient physiologic parameters. 130. The medicament dispensing system according to claim 129, wherein the parameters include those selected from the group consisting of: weight, blood pressure readings by associated devices (wireless or picture of display with outward facing camera) and others. 131. A medicament dispensing system, including: a housing comprising a carousel, the carousel including a plurality of removable containers where each container provides a repository for medicaments therein; a retrieval probe, said probe located within the confines of the housing and operatively moving between an access port to at least one container and the pre-dispense port, said probe releasably engaging a medicament in one of the plurality of containers via said access port, retrieving the medicament from the container and delivering the medicament to a dispensing location; and a dispense location monitor (e.g., monitoring patient access to the dispensing location, a camera for confirming medicaments being retrieved, etc.), wherein upon detection of a plurality of at least N successive failures (e.g., 1 - 7 days' worth) of a patient to collected dispensed dosage(s) (e.g., a days' worth), the dispensing system places itself in a vacation mode whereby subsequent dispensing is disabled unless/until a patient or caregiver initiates restarting of the dispense. 132.
